exemplar.title: M25 #5: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #5: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, clear focus on Boxâs claim with coherent line of reasoning
Effective integration of two Areas of Knowledge with well-explained, concrete real-world examples
Clear definitions of key terms (âwrongâ and âusefulâ) that support the conceptual framework
weaknesses: Repetitive treatment of counter-claims that limits deeper critical evaluation
Occasional abrupt transitions and under-developed linkages between sections
Insufficient formalization of models (e.g. missing equations and goodness-of-fit metrics)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay aligns closely with the 7â8 band descriptors: it maintains a sustained focus on the title, integrates both mathematics and human sciences purposefully, and offers clear, coherent arguments supported by specific examples. The student demonstrates awareness of alternative perspectives and considers implications, meriting a high-end band score. However, the repetition of rebuttals, abrupt transitions, and lack of full formalization of models (e.g. missing explicit equations and RÂ² discussion) prevent it from achieving the sustained sophistication and depth required for the top band. Assigning 8 marks accurately reflects these strengths and remaining analytical gaps.

annotations.comment: The regression discussion would benefit from formalizing the model: include the equation $$y=mx+b$$ to show the mathematical relationship between variables.
You describe HeckscherâOhlinâs assumptions but do not critically evaluate their impact on the modelâs predictive reliability; deepen this analysis.
Consider discussing a goodness-of-fit metric such as the coefficient of determination ($$R^2$$) to strengthen the evaluation of the regression modelâs usefulness.
The figure and accompanying table are clear, but you should explicitly link the observed data dispersion to discussion of the modelâs margin of error to reinforce your argument.
The sentence truncates abruptly; ensure each claim is fully developed or complete the thought to maintain clarity of analysis.
The transition from regression to logistic models is abrupt. Add a linking sentence to contextualize why you are shifting focus to growth-rate models.
Introduce the logistic function formally with an equation (e.g. $$f(t)=\frac{K}{1+Ae^{-rt}}$$) to ground your discussion in mathematical precision.
The section on logistic modeling repeats similar points about margin of error; consolidate overlapping ideas to deepen analysis rather than reiterate.
The introduction to human sciences is generic; specify the particular economic model you will analyze to provide clear direction for this AOK section.
The use of empirical trade data linking the USA and Bangladesh to HeckscherâOhlin is effective and grounds your discussion in a concrete real-world example.
Precise definitions of âwrongâ and âusefulâ clarify the essayâs conceptual foundation, aiding the reader in distinguishing key terms central to the argument.
The thesis is somewhat vague and lacks a structured roadmap; explicitly outline how each AOK will be addressed to improve coherence and guide the reader.
The student effectively rephrases the prescribed title and frames the core inquiry, demonstrating clear engagement with Boxâs claim from the outset.
Introducing counterclaims earlier in the human sciences section will help frame a more balanced evaluation of model usefulness.
The rhetorical question engages the reader but needs a clearer link to the ensuing example â explicitly state how dual interpretations lead to different uses.
Define what constitutes an âacceptable margin of errorâ in evaluating model usefulness to give your argument a more rigorous evaluative criterion.
The application of a biological logistic model to business contexts is creative but speculative; strengthen it with empirical evidence or refine the analogyâs scope.
The ArrowâDebreu critique lists standard criticisms but does not tie them back to Boxâs trade-off between wrongness and usefulness; make this connection explicit.
References to the RBC and OLG models are descriptive; include a concrete example of each to illustrate how they demonstrate Boxâs concept of âuseful despite wrong.â
The conclusion effectively restates the thesis but lacks depth: address a substantive counterclaim to demonstrate higher-level critical evaluation.
Consider reflecting on the broader implications of interpretation-based usefulness for the scientific community to enrich the final evaluative stance.
Adding a concise roadmap in the introduction outlining each AOK discussion will improve navigability and coherence throughout the essay.
Strengthen coherence by adding explicit transition sentences between the mathematics and human sciences sections to guide the reader.
Discussing alternative variables omitted by the HeckscherâOhlin model, such as political factors, would demonstrate more critical awareness of its limitations.
Consider incorporating residualâplot analysis or other diagnostic tools to deepen the regression evaluation beyond the trendline alone.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
